text:
There once was a small town nestled between mountains. It wasn't big; just a few streets lined with quaint homes and lively shops. Every Easter, the town would hold something special—an egg hunt filled with surprises.

One year, a big oak tree nestled right in the middle of town. Its branches were festooned with colorful eggs. There was just one slight problem: the tree was dead.

Word spread fast. Children were disappointed. Adults felt nostalgic.

That afternoon, some brave souls climbed up onto the cracked oak. They found dozens of beautifully painted eggs nestled among the decayed branches.

Children rejoiced. They decorated their nests overnight.

Come Easter morning, the townsfolk descended onto their hometown treasures. They giggled happily as they gathered ovals, rabbits, and even a squirrel.

However, just as they settled down for breakfast, someone exclaimed!

An incredible sight unfolded. The largest egg lay atop a statue at the edge of town—a majestic eagle. Its feathers shimmered. A tiny
